title: Zachary karate (integration_bench config): full-QA integration DEGRADES ARI 0.77 → 0.15 (dARI=-0.62) — CONTRADICTS with remedy in companion hub-distance cert

observation.summary: Honest failure report: integration_bench's default QA tuple construction degrades Zachary karate community detection from baseline ARI=0.7717 to ARI=0.1528 (dARI=-0.6189). Root cause diagnosed 2026-04-05: the (b=degree, e=core_number) encoding collapses both degree-hubs into identical invariant space, destroying the hub-proximity signal karate ground truth depends on. The remedy is the hub-distance descriptor in karate_hub_distance_qa.py / karate_spectral_qa_fingerprint.py, which recovers the signal via the 'e' invariant alone. This cert is a CONTRADICTS record of the suboptimal application; the corresponding remedy is certed separately as qa.cert.empirical.karate_spectral_qa_fingerprint_e_single_coord.v1.

parent_cert.claim: QA invariants improve graph community detection — this cert CONTRADICTS the claim for karate under the default tuple encoding

evidence.description: Spectral baseline on Zachary karate (k=2, in integration_bench config)
Full-QA ARI (C, X, G, F invariants appended to spectral features)
Every individual invariant (X, G, F) also reduces ARI to ~0.15
evidence.value: there is no single invariant that preserves baseline performance — the failure is structural for this graph

evidence.description: ROOT CAUSE (per 2026-04-05 diagnostic): karate ground truth reflects hub-proximity; integration_bench's tuple construction maps both hubs to identical invariant space via (b=degree, e=core_number), causing collapse. This is a suboptimal APPLICATION of QA, not a QA failure — see companion cert qa.cert.empirical.karate_hub_distance_qa_partial.v1 for the remedial descriptor.
evidence.value: map-best-to-QA principle: the right QA descriptor for hub-dominated graphs is integer hub-distance, not centrality

validation_checks.description: observation.source is a known source type
verdict is one of {CONSISTENT, CONTRADICTS, PARTIAL, INCONCLUSIVE}

fail_ledger.description: Integration bench's (b=degree, e=core_number) tuple construction collapses both degree-hubs into identical invariant space, destroying the hub-proximity signal that drives karate ground truth.
fail_ledger.evidence: dARI = -0.6189 vs spectral baseline; every QA invariant falls to ARI ~0.15
fail_ledger.remedy: Use (b=dist_to_hub0, e=dist_to_hub1) hub-distance construction — see karate_hub_distance_qa.py and karate_spectral_qa_fingerprint.py; the 'e' invariant alone recovers ARI 0.77 vs ground truth.
